facts: 51 splits across 11 datasets × 3 shift types; explicit covariate-vs-concept-shift labels; Gui et al. NeurIPS 2022.
hypotheses: Methods that win on covariate shift also win on concept shift within the same dataset.
Conclusions drawn on the synthetic GOOD datasets (Motif, CMNIST) transfer to the real ones (HIV, Arxiv).
intuitions: GOOD-2 or successor 2024-25; expansions beyond binary covariate/concept.
unknowns: Whether covariate-vs-concept is the right partition, or conflates finer distinctions, cannot be determined from the suite alone.
The synthetic sub-datasets violate the natural-shift criterion, which the suite-level framing glosses over.
